message:
🌱 Add kubescape: Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego. mission (#775)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubescape-1397-would-like-to-see-cilium-crds-recognized-by-kubescape-rego",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubescape: Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.",
+    "description": "Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check Kubescape installation",
+        "description": "Verify your Kubescape installation:\n```bash\nkubescape version\n```\nInstall if needed: `curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubescape/kubescape/master/install.sh | /bin/bash`. Kubescape can run as a CLI tool or be deployed in-cluster."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubescape configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubescape configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape\nkubectl get configmap -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubescape\n```\nKubernetes offers a simple NetworkPolicy CRD but is limited in functionality and doesn't completely provide all the necessary controls and policy capabilities that most CNIs would. This is a base functionality."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.",
+        "description": "Yes, but I am not sure what controls. Do you mean those that check if a\nworkload has a bound network policy?\n\nOn Mon, Feb 12, 2024 at 7:34\u202fAM David Wertenteil ***@***.***>\nwrote:\n\n>  <https://github.com/slashben> Yossi has generating cilium and\n> calico NP up his sleeve. Maybe we should add the\n```yaml\napiVersion: \"cilium.io/v2\"\nkind: CiliumNetworkPolicy\nmetadata:\n  name: deny-all\n  namespace: default\nspec:\n  endpointSelector:\n    matchLabels: {}\n  ingress: []\n  egress: []\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubescape -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubescape\nkubectl get events -n kubescape --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Would like to see Cilium CRDs recognized by Kubescape/rego.\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Yes, but I am not sure what controls. Do you mean those that check if a\nworkload has a bound network policy?\n\nOn Mon, Feb 12, 2024 at 7:34\u202fAM David Wertenteil ***@***.***>\nwrote:\n\n>  <https://github.com/slashben> Yossi has generating cilium and\n> calico NP up his sleeve. Maybe we should add the controls support as part\n> of that epic.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: \"cilium.io/v2\"\nkind: CiliumNetworkPolicy\nmetadata:\n  name: deny-all\n  namespace: default\nspec:\n  endpointSelector:\n    matchLabels: {}\n  ingress: []\n  egress: []"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kubescape",
+      "incubating",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kubescape"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Ingress",
+      "Namespace",
+      "Networkpolicy"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kubescape/kubescape/issues/1397",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kubescape/kubescape"
+    },
+    "reactions": 4,
+    "comments": 11,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "kubescape"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kubescape installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
